steps.title: Parallel lines have equal slopes — read slope directly from standard form
steps.content:
For $ax + by = c$ the slope is $-a/b$, no algebra needed.
Line $j$: $4x + 5y = 55$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -4/5$.
Line $k$: $24x + ry = 15$ $\Rightarrow$ slope $= -24/r$.
Set equal: $-4/5 = -24/r$ $\Rightarrow$ $r = 30$.

steps.title: Plug the y-intercept point into line k
steps.content:
Line $k$ is $24x + 30y = 15$. The point $(0, b)$ lies on it, so substitute $x = 0$:
$$30b = 15 \implies b = \frac{1}{2}$$
The answer is $\boxed{0.5}$ (or $1/2$).
